title: Today's Tech Brief: Company CEO flooded file share with smut, called for help after he deleted it
summary: A CEO's NSFW file share blunder, a missing school iPad's unexpected YouTube debut, and tech news in plain language.

body.text: I've got a couple of stories that are making me scratch my head today. Let's dive into some tech news that's got a bit of everything: security slip-ups, unexpected YouTube appearances, and what happens when you press delete. These aren't exactly earth-shattering events, but they're definitely entertaining and worth a quick chat.

First off, there's a company CEO who seems to have forgotten that his file share wasn't his personal playground. According to a report from The Register, he flooded the company's file share with, ahem, not-safe-for-work content. But here's the kicker: after he deleted it, he called for help. I'm guessing his IT team had a field day with that one. The question on my mind is, what made him think deleting it would solve the problem? It's like trying to un-send an email – we all know that's just not how it works.

My quick read: today's tech stories are less about one giant thunderclap and more about a lively weather pattern. A little platform drama here, a little artificial intelligence irony there, and suddenly the industry has enough material for a very caffeinated morning show.

sources.title: Company CEO flooded file share with smut, called for help after he deleted it
